message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Our main aim is to estimate the end of the first wave epidemic of COVID-19 outbreak in mainland China. We developed mathematical models to predict reasonable bounds on the date of end of the COVID-19 epidemics in mainland China with strong quarantine and testing measures for a sufficiently long time. We used reported data in China from January 20, 2020 to April 9, 2020. We firstly used a deterministic approach to obtain a formula to compute the probability distribution of the extinction date by combining the models and continuous-time Markov processes. Then we present the individual based model (IMB) simulations to compare the result by deterministic approach and show the absolute difference between the estimated cumulative probability distribution computed by simulations and formula. We provide the predictions of the end of the first wave epidemic for different fractions
                  <jats:italic>f</jats:italic>
                  of asymptomatic infectious that become reported symptomatic infectious.
                </jats:p>
title: Estimating the end of the first wave of epidemic for COVID-19 outbreak in mainland China

reference.article-title: A primer on stochastic epidemic models: Formulation, numerical simulation, and analysis
